rationale: Each entry was flagged by 3+ independent US tech-resume guides as weak / vague / templated. Sources: The Pragmatic Engineer (Gergely Orosz), Lenny's Newsletter, MIT CAPD, Harvard FAS Mignone Center, Yale Office of Career Strategy, Workology, Tealhq, ResumeWorded, IGotAnOffer, Toptal, Resufit, Recruiter.com, Language Log, plus academic resume linguistics literature. See research-notes.md for citations.
match_semantics: Case-insensitive whole-phrase match with word boundaries on both sides (regex \b<phrase>\b). Implementations that use bare substring matching MUST tighten to whole-phrase to avoid false positives such as 'various' inside 'cross-functional' or 'etc.' inside structured prose.
policy: Each phrases[] occurrence reduces banned_phrase_cleanliness by 1.0 (floor 0). soft_overuse[] entries flag in notes only — they deduct 0.5 if the SAME phrase occurs ≥2 times in the draft (floor 0). The reviewer turn surfaces the offending bullet via banned_phrase_hits in pipe-separated format: '<phrase>|<bullet text>'.
